I have all the information I need: ## Review Summary for Issue 815d2ab7-5b29-40e4-8f54-01f9399b9727 **Status:** No significant issues found in the reviewed changes. ### Acceptance Criteria Verification All success criteria from the issue description are **satisfied**: ✅ **Image rendering**: `.png`, `.svg`, and `.jpg` files render as inline images - **Implementation:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.tsx` (lines 10-20) - **Registration:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts` (line 78) ✅ **Python syntax highlighting**: `.py` files render with Python syntax highlighting via `TextCodeRenderer` - **Implementation:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.tsx` (line 29: `return 'python'`) ✅ **Renderer ordering**: Image extensions matched before markdown catch-all - **Evidence:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts` (lines 76-86 for image, line 112-121 for markdown catch-all) ✅ **Script extensions**: `.py`, `.sh`, `.js`, `.ts` matched before catch-all - **Evidence:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts` (line 102) ✅ **Test coverage**: All new renderer paths have unit test coverage in `renderers.test.tsx` - **Evidence:** `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx` (lines 62-109) - Includes tests for: `.png`, `.svg`, `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.gif`, `.webp`, `.py`, `.sh`, `.js`, `.ts` ✅ **npm-ci gate**: Passes (vitest, ESLint, build) - **Evidence:** Gate run `7d5b51e5-e18e-4d8c-a1fc-0dc89e382995` shows `status: "passed"`, `exit_code: 0`, completed on 2026-05-15T21:00:20 ### Prior Review Feedback Resolution The prior code-review gate (run `36d4fffc-e6b9-435f-b3b1-0323a2b00d3e`) **failed** at commit `3efaaef` with one blocking issue: **Blocker:** Missing test coverage for `.jpeg`, `.gif`, `.webp` image formats **Resolution:** ✅ **ADDRESSED** in commit `96969d1` - Added 15 lines of test coverage in `renderers.test.tsx` (lines 77-90) - Tests verify all three formats route to the image renderer ### Architecture & Code Quality ✅ **Architecture boundaries**: Changes remain within the Web UI renderer layer (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/*`). No cross-layer violations. ✅ **Separation of concerns**: Renderer selection is declarative via registry pattern. No new imperative branching introduced. ✅ **Functional paradigm**: Pure functions for extension matching and renderer selection (`matchesExtension`, `pickRenderer`). ✅ **Error handling**: No new error paths introduced. Image rendering follows existing URL generation pattern with proper null handling. ✅ **Testing**: Comprehensive unit test coverage includes: - `ImageRenderer.test.tsx`: 6 tests covering URL generation, commit propagation, alt text fallbacks - `renderers.test.tsx`: 24 tests covering all format matching and registry ordering ### Technical Implementation Notes - The implementation uses file extension matching only, not the optional `doc_type` field mentioned in success criteria — this is acceptable as the criteria explicitly say "optionally" - Additional image formats (`.jpeg`, `.gif`, `.webp`) were included beyond the minimum requirements (`.png`, `.svg`, `.jpg`) and now have proper test coverage - Image renderer correctly uses `getRawDocumentUrl` for both issue-scoped and standalone document paths - Syntax highlighting properly maps extensions to Prism language identifiers ### Gate Status - **npm-ci**: ✅ PASSED (May 15, 21:00:07 UTC) - **code-review**: ❌ FAILED (May 15, 21:00:23 UTC) — but blocking issue was resolved in subsequent commit `96969d1` **Final Verdict:** The current state (commit `96969d1`) fully satisfies all acceptance criteria and addresses the prior review blocking issue. No architectural violations, correctness issues, or technical debt introduced.The direct shell checks are blocked here, so I’m confirming current state from the repo files and gate artifacts instead. ## Acceptance criteria - **Met:** image files are routed before the catch-all and render inline. `pickRenderer` matches `png/svg/jpg/jpeg/gif/webp` in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-85`, and `ImageRenderer` renders an actual `` using the raw document URL in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.tsx:10-20`. - **Met:** Python and other script files are routed to syntax-highlighted code rendering. The registry sends `.py/.sh/.js/.ts` to `text-code` in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:100-109`, and `TextCodeRenderer` maps them to Prism languages in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.tsx:26-33`. - **Met:** the renderer ordering is correct. The `image` and `text-code` entries appear before the markdown catch-all in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-121`, and `pickRenderer` returns the first match in `index.ts:124-127`. - **Met:** the required unit coverage exists in `renderers.test.tsx`. Image routes are covered at `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:62-90`, script routes at `renderers.test.tsx:92-109`, and the catch-all ordering at `renderers.test.tsx:133-136`. - **Met:** the available `npm-ci` run passed: `.jit/gate-runs/7d5b51e5-e18e-4d8c-a1fc-0dc89e382995/result.json:1-14`. ## Prior review feedback - The previous review failed on missing coverage for `.jpeg/.gif/.webp` routes; that failure is recorded in `.jit/gate-runs/36d4fffc-e6b9-435f-b3b1-0323a2b00d3e/result.json:1-18`. - That issue is now resolved: the missing cases were added in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:77-90`. ## Architecture and separation of concerns - The change stays entirely in the Web UI renderer layer: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts`, `ImageRenderer.tsx`, `TextCodeRenderer.tsx`, and renderer tests. I did not find any spillover into Rust domain, storage, command, CLI, or output layers. ## Functional paradigm - Renderer selection remains declarative and side-effect free: extension matching is isolated in `matchesExtension` in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:55-62`, and dispatch is a pure `find(...)` over the registry in `index.ts:124-127`. - `TextCodeRenderer` keeps language resolution in a pure helper (`resolveLanguage`) at `web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.tsx:26-33`. ## Correctness and invariants - The intended file types cannot fall through to markdown because the registry order is correct in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-121`. - `ImageRenderer` constructs the raw asset URL consistently for issue-scoped and standalone documents in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.tsx:10-12`, and supplies accessible fallback alt text in `ImageRenderer.tsx:16-19`. - No code in this patch touches Rust-side invariants around atomic writes, event logging, gate transitions, assignee/label parsing, or PID handling. ## Safety - No Rust workspace safety surface changed, and nothing in the reviewed files introduces `unsafe` code or PID-sensitive logic. ## Testing - Route coverage is comprehensive in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:62-109`. - Renderer-specific behavior is covered in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.test.tsx:41-106`. - I could not rerun `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` or the web commands directly because shell execution is blocked in this environment, so I relied on the provided gate artifacts. The available `npm-ci` artifact is green at `.jit/gate-runs/7d5b51e5-e18e-4d8c-a1fc-0dc89e382995/result.json:1-14`, and the only change after the failed review is added test coverage in `renderers.test.tsx:77-90`. ## Error handling - No new fallible workflow or silent failure path was introduced. The new renderer follows the existing `getRawDocumentUrl(...)` path construction pattern in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.tsx:10-12`. ## CLI contract - No CLI commands or `--json` surfaces changed. ## Documentation - No public API surface changed in this patch, so I found no documentation blocker in scope. ## Jit issue management - The issue has no prerequisites to satisfy: `.jit/issues/815d2ab7-5b29-40e4-8f54-01f9399b9727.json:8-12`. - The stored issue metadata still shows the earlier failed code-review status at `.jit/issues/815d2ab7-5b29-40e4-8f54-01f9399b9727.json:13-23`, but the underlying finding from that run has been addressed in the current code. VERDICT: PASS --- Gate result: PASSED
